title: Programme Executive | Welfare Home
description:
• Design, strategize, and implement a dynamic calendar of daily, weekly, and monthly activities and social outings tailored to resident's interests and needs.
  • Oversee all operational logistics for seamless programme delivery, including venue setup, material preparation, and attendance tracking.
  • Collaborate with external trainers, volunteers, and community partners to deliver specialized wellness sessions and workshops.
  • Continuously evaluate programme effectiveness through surveys and feedback to ensure high quality and positive impact.
  • Proactively recruit senior members and maintain accurate and up-to-date participant records.
  • Build and nurture strong, trusting relationships with residents, their families, and community partners to foster a supportive environment.
  • Liaise with corporations, volunteers, and agencies to enhance programme offerings and secure resources.
  • Stay informed on community developments and integrate innovative measures to support ageing well.
  • Conduct regular programme reviews and prepare detailed monthly reports for internal stakeholders and funders.
  • Collect and analyze participant data and feedback to measure satisfaction, assess outcomes, and drive continuous improvement.
  • Support the development of grant proposals and outcome reports to secure funding and demonstrate programme value.


Job Requirements

  • Diploma/Degree in any discipline
  • Min 2 year of relevant experience
  • Able to work independently and collaboratively while managing multiple tasks and priorities effectively


Check out http://abercare.sg/jobs-vacancies/ for details.


Interested applicant please email your detailed resume to career@abercare.sg

postedCompany.description:
We Connect Opportunities to People

Aber Care is a recruitment agency specializing in connecting talented professionals with meaningful career opportunities in both healthcare and early childhood education.

In healthcare, our years of experience in the industry have given us deep insights into its manpower needs. We support hospitals, community hospitals, nursing homes, hospices, and senior care centers by placing skilled professionals such as staff nurses, nursing aides, nurse managers, healthcare assistants, and live-in caregivers. Beyond full-time and locum employment, we also provide foreign healthcare placement to ensure a strong and sustainable workforce.

In early childhood education, we partner with preschools to recruit dedicated educators, including preschool teachers, assistant teachers, and allied infant educarers, ensuring a nurturing learning environment for young children.

With expertise in both healthcare and education, we are committed to matching passionate professionals with rewarding careers while helping organizations build strong, dedicated teams in these essential fields.

EA Licence No. 18C9070
